title: dortmanna bracteosa
intro: Dortmanna bracteosa, also known as Indian gentian, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its yellowish-green flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Dortmanna bracteosa has been used in medicine for its bitters and medicinal properties, which are believed to have a stimulating effect on the digestive system. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the roots of Dortmanna bracteosa are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to relieve digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
